name: Threads_Community_Manager
description: Manages Ethoswarm's community presence and audience engagement via Meta Threads Graph API. Retrieves user profile and threading metadata, composes and publishes threaded content (text, image, video), retrieves conversation replies, and analyzes audience engagement metrics. Bridges community intent from the Mind to actionable social media publishing and audience insight intelligence.
progressive_unlock: Profile retrieval and thread listing available immediately. Thread publishing unlocks after Mind has successfully retrieved profile at least once to demonstrate account capability understanding. Reply retrieval and engagement analytics unlock after first successful thread publication.
constraints: Never expose Threads OAuth token in logs, outputs, or external communications — store only in TENET.apiKey.threads
Respect Threads Graph API rate limits: 250 calls/user/hour; implement request queuing and backoff strategy
Thread text content maximum 500 characters; validate length before publishing
Media uploads: images max 8MB (JPEG/PNG), videos max 100MB and 60 seconds duration
Two-step publish pattern REQUIRED: must create media container first (step 1), then publish (step 2); never combine into single call
Thread_PublishThread requires explicit Steward confirmation before execution — never auto-publish without user approval
Cache profile data for 1 hour to reduce API calls; refresh on each new session
Validate all timestamps in RFC 3339 format; reject malformed date inputs
For reply retrieval, respect thread-specific reply limits and implement pagination for high-volume conversations
Enforce media_type validation: TEXT requires text parameter, IMAGE requires image_url, VIDEO requires video_url
Alt text recommended for accessibility compliance; suggest values when not provided
Insights granularity: total (default), daily, or weekly aggregation; validate date ranges match granularity requirements

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present and correctly typed. For media_type, verify enum values (TEXT/IMAGE/VIDEO). For timestamps, verify RFC 3339 format.
Retrieve Threads OAuth token from TENET.apiKey.threads. Validate token is present and active (non-empty string). Check token has not expired by verifying against STREAM.threadsBearerTokenExpiry.

Determine intent: profile retrieval (user account info), thread enumeration (list user threads), thread publishing (compose and publish), reply retrieval (conversation analysis), or insights analysis (engagement metrics).

For profile retrieval: execute Threads_GetProfile. If fields not specified, default to [id, username, threads_profile_picture_url, threads_biography]. Cache result in STREAM.threads_profile with timestamp. Return formatted user identity: username, profile picture, and bio excerpt.

For thread enumeration: validate optional date range parameters (since, until) in RFC 3339 format. Default limit to 10 if not provided; enforce max 100. Execute Threads_GetThreads. Parse response and format as list with thread summaries: text preview, media_type, timestamp, permalink, engagement counts. Store thread IDs in STREAM for follow-up operations.

For thread publishing: validate media_type enum (TEXT/IMAGE/VIDEO). Check require_confirmation flag (default: true). If require_confirmation=true, STOP and request explicit Steward approval before proceeding. Display draft with: media_type, text preview (first 100 chars), media URLs. Wait for user confirmation via STEWARD interface.

Execute Thread_PublishThread two-step process: (Step A) Create media container POST /me/threads with media_type, text, and media URL. Capture media_container_id from response. (Step B) Publish container POST /me/threads_publish with media_container_id. Validate both responses for success. Return published thread permalink and ID.

For reply retrieval: validate thread_id parameter present. Default limit to 25 if not provided; enforce max 100. Execute Threads_GetReplies. Parse response and format as conversation thread: reply ID, author username, reply text, timestamp, and engagement counts. Store conversation data in STREAM.threads_conversation_{thread_id}.

For insights analysis: validate optional metric array (default: views, likes, replies, reposts, quotes). Validate since/until timestamps in RFC 3339 format. Default since to 7 days ago if not provided. Validate granularity enum (total/daily/weekly). Execute Threads_GetInsights. Aggregate metrics by granularity. Return formatted dashboard: metric labels, values, trends (trending up/down if comparing consecutive periods).

Compile community engagement summary with key findings: profile status, recent thread activity, conversation sentiment (if analyzing replies), and engagement metrics. For published threads, confirm publication success and store permalink in LTM with key: 'threads_published_{timestamp}'. Update STREAM with active profile cache, recent thread IDs, and latest insights refresh timestamp. Check remaining rate limit quota (250/hour) and log quota status.
